channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Florida's Walter Clayton Jr. recalls final Houston play in title game, admits surprise Cougars didn't get shot
item.description: Florida Gators star Walter Clayton Jr. admitted he was surprised Houston never got a shot off during its final possession of the title game.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chess, not checkers: Knicks rest core players ahead of potential playoff matchup vs. Detroit Pistons
item.description: New York Knicks head coach Tom Thibodeau seems to be playing chess while the “rest” of the National Basketball Association is playing checkers, as he has ruled out key players for their potential playoff matchup vs. the Detroit Pistons.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Carr, Local 4

item.title: Fred Couples shoots 71 at the Masters. So much for the 65-year-old thinking he no longer belongs
item.description: Fred Couples shot a 1-under 71 in the opening round of the Masters on Thursday, the 65-year-old former champion proving to himself as much as anyone else that he still belongs.
item.dc:creator: Dave Skretta, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: SF Giants’ Hayden Birdsong takes bullpen role into first game at Yankee Stadium
item.description: Right-hander Hayden Birdsong is temporarily in a new role as a reliever and thriving as the Giants take a 9-3 record into Yankee Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Jerry McDonald, The Mercury News

item.title: Jokic has 32nd triple-double to lead Nuggets past Kings 124-116 in Denver's 1st game without Malone
item.description: SACRAMENTO (AP) — Nikola Jokic had his 32nd triple-double of the season with 20 points, 12 rebounds and 11 assists and Denver beat the Sacramento Kings 124-116 on Wednesday night in the Nuggets' first game since head coach Michael Malone was fired.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL WAGAMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mikheyev, Nazar and Knight lead the Blackhawks to a 3-1 win over the Penguins
item.description: Ilya Mikheyev scored twice, Frank Nazar added a goal and Spencer Knight stopped 28 shots as the Chicago Blackhawks snapped a five-game skid with a 3-1 win over the Pittsburgh Penguins on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Coby White scores 37, Josh Giddey adds 23 as Bulls beat Hornets 131-117 for 3rd straight win
item.description: Coby White scored 37 points, shooting 6 of 8 from 3-point range, Josh Giddey added 23 points, 10 rebounds and eight assists and the Chicago Bulls rolled past the Charlotte Hornets 131-117 on Sunday for their third straight win.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Justin Turner's error allows Fernando Tatis Jr. to score go-ahead run in Padres' 8-7 win over Cubs
item.description: Luis Arraez had four hits, Fernando Tatis Jr. scored the go-ahead run in the ninth inning when first baseman Justin Turner couldn't make the catch to finish a double play, and the San Diego Padres beat the Chicago Cubs 8-7 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Alex Ovechkin breaks Wayne Gretzky's NHL career goals record by scoring his 895th
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Alex Ovechkin fired just about the perfect version of his signature one-timer that has defined his remarkable career. When the puck hit the net, it made him the top goal scorer in NHL history.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Florida makes push all the way to NCAA title game, set to face Houston
item.description: Florida has gone from being picked to finish sixth in the rugged Southeastern Conference to pushing all the way to the final Monday night of the season.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Clayton leads Florida to NCAA title game, scoring 34 points in 79-73 victory over SEC rival Auburn
item.description: Set to face Houston in the title game Monday night at the Alamodome, the Gators finally are getting a shot at another national championship.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Utah HC stays alive with 4-1 victory over Winnipeg
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club is probably not going to make the NHL playoffs, but they are not going down without a fight. Karel Vejmelka made 32 saves for Utah in a 4-1 win against the the top team in the NHL, the Winnipeg Jets, at Delta Center on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Ian Happ plays in 1,000th major league game
item.description: Ian Happ played in his 1,000th major league game on Saturday — all with the Chicago Cubs. It's that last part that makes it special for the outfielder.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bulls Head Coach Billy Donovan joins Carmelo Anthony, Sue Bird and others in Basketball Hall of Fame class
item.description: Carmelo Anthony and Dwight Howard are going into the Basketball Hall of Fame later this year, not once but twice. And LeBron James and Chris Paul are part of the group that’s headed to the Hall as well, even before their playing careers end.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, WGN 9

item.title: Cleveland's José Ramírez hits 3 homers and claims another piece of Guardians history
item.description: José Ramírez hit three homers and carried the Guardians to a victory Friday night. The slugger claimed another place in Cleveland baseball history in the process.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Real Salt Lake hosts winless champs LA Galaxy in crucial western showdown
item.description: Real Salt Lake (2-4-0, T-11th place Western Conference) returns home Saturday afternoon when they host reigning MLS Cup Champions LA Galaxy at America First Field. RSL is coming off a road loss on Saturday night and hope to get their season back on track against a struggling LA side.
item.dc:creator: Mattey Casey, ABC 4

item.title: Vucevic, White, Giddey lead the post-season bound Bulls past the Trail Blazers, 118-113
item.description: Nikola Vucevic and Coby White each had 31 points, Josh Giddey had a triple-double and the post-season bound Chicago Bulls beat the Portland Trail Blazers 118-113 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Teams battle it out in NCAA Women's Final Four
item.description: The South Carolina Gamecocks are headed back to the NCAA Championship game to fight for their second straight national title.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Parker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pacers run over Jazz in fourth quarter in 140-112 win
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz hung with the Indiana Pacers for three quarters, trailing by just four points going into the fourth. But Indiana outscored Utah in the final quarter, 42-18 en route to a 140-112 win. The Jazz have lost eight in a row and 18 of its last 19 games.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Kings score three times in third period to beat Utah
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club's playoff chances are growing slimmer with each loss, and it looks like it's going to take a miraculous turn of events to get in now.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Red Rocks advance to NCAA regional finals
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The University of Utah gymnastics team is moving on to the NCAA regional finals after scoring a 197.625 on the first night of competition, tying with Denver University. Both the Utes and the Pioneers advance to Saturday's regional finals along with UCLA and Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Women's Final Four teams touch the court for shootarounds ahead of tournament
item.description: During press conferences, the teams expressed excitement and an eagerness to fight for their spot in the championship.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Parker, WFLA, NBC
